subject: Anthropology
title: Kinship Terminology
description: A quick win: understand Kinship Terminology.

hook.text: English has one word for cousin. Some languages have eight. Your kinship vocabulary shapes how you see your family.

buildup.text: Kinship terminology systems classify relatives into categories, and each system reflects different social priorities.

discovery.text: There are six major systems: Hawaiian, Eskimo, Sudanese, Omaha, Crow, and Iroquois, each grouping relatives differently.

twist.text: Terminology isn't just labels. Calling someone 'brother' versus 'cousin' changes your obligations toward them.

climax.text: Kinship terms reveal hidden social logic. They show who matters, who helps, and who you must respect or avoid.

punchline.text: How you name relatives shapes how you treat them.

quiz.question: Why do kinship terminology systems matter?
options: They shape social obligations and reveal who matters in a society
They only exist in written languages
